description: The dashboard sections are supposed to be reorderable via drag-and-drop, but dragging a section and dropping it on another position often does nothing. The sections stay in their original order even after repeated attempts.
expectedOutcome: Dragging a dashboard section (e.g. 'Key Metrics') and dropping it onto another section (e.g. 'Recent Activity') should reorder them — the dragged section should appear at the new position.
hint: Look at the drag-and-drop handlers in DashboardBuilder.jsx. Verify that drag-over marks a valid drop target, and that section reordering updates state immutably.
solution:
There are two bugs in src/components/DashboardBuilder.jsx:

Bug 1 — handleDragOver did not allow dropping:
The HTML5 drag-and-drop API requires e.preventDefault() in dragover to mark a valid drop target. Without this, onDrop may not fire reliably.

Fix: add e.preventDefault() inside handleDragOver.

Bug 2 — section reorder must update state immutably:
The reorder logic must create a new array from previous state before splice, otherwise React may not detect updates correctly.

Fix: in reorder logic, use functional state update and clone with const reordered = [...prevSections] before mutating.
